Bump MoRI to v1.1.0 in the ROCm Dockerfile. This release introduces torch-free pybind, host/device separation (zero hipcc at install time), and runtime NIC auto-detection.
MoRI release note: https://github.com/ROCm/mori/releases/tag/v1.1.0

sglang.title: [diffusion] model: Properly validate device for Mistral 3 attention
sglang.author: avjves
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
PR #22423 changed it so that Mistral 3 (Used in Flux2) uses cuDNN attention by default, if the device type is cuda. AMD HW however also reports the device type as cuda, but does not support cuDNN attention. This change broke AMD support for Flux2.

Changes the check in Mistr

sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Fix: Normalize tool message content from array format to string before applying chat template
Problem
Per the OpenAI API specification, the content field of a tool message supports two formats:

String: "content": "10525"
Array of content parts: "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "10525"}]

Follow-up to #22919. After switching get_loads_communicator to watching mode, the scheduler always receives include=["all"] (since concurrent callers share one request). This made the include query parameter a no-op — callers always got all sections regardless of what they requested.

sglang.title: [Ray] Support multi-replica serving by making scheduler actor names unique
sglang.author: xyuzh
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Multi-replica SGLang serving on Ray (e.g., Ray Serve with num_replicas > 1)
is currently broken due to a SchedulerActor name collision.
The problem. The scheduler actor name is derived from node IP, PP rank,
and TP rank:
sglang_scheduler_node<ip>_pp0_tp0
When two replicas share a node and

sglang.title: [Ray] Bind scheduler actors to GPU-local NUMA node
sglang.author: xyuzh
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Ray actors are spawned by Ray's raylet, not via multiprocessing.spawn, so the numactl subprocess-wrapping path that SGLANG_NUMA_BIND_V2 uses (configure_subprocess in python/sglang/srt/utils/numa_utils.py) never applies to them. With the default SGLANG_NUMA_BIND_V2=True, scheduler actors wer

After shared expert fusion is enabled for Qwen3.5 models (as in #20736 ), MXFP4 model hits an issue: the shared expert in the checkpoint is based on BF16 but current weight loading can only treat it as MXFP4 which is the dtype of routed experts. So before either online quantization is rea

Under SGLANG_USE_CUDA_IPC_TRANSPORT=1, each non-source TP rank was creating a full CUDA context on the producer GPU (~500 MiB per rank, ~1.6 GiB at

sglang.title: feat: add coordinated checkpoint prefetch for network filesystem loading
sglang.author: janbernloehr
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Closes #20842
Motivation
When multiple DP ranks on the same node load the same checkpoint via mmap (e.g. DP-attention), each rank independently page-faults all safetensors files over NFS/Lustre. With N ranks this causes N × checkpoint_size bytes of redundant network I/O.
Note: this is orthogonal to

I'll run through an example using Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B with se

Sending a request with min_tokens to the toy_proxy_server.py currently fails on P as it receives a request with max_tokens=1 and min_tokens>1, crashing on validation.
This small patch allows to skip min_tokens sending to P, while forwarding that to D only.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix bench_serve UTF-8 decode crash on split multi-byte chars
vllm.author: he-yufeng
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
StreamedResponseHandler.add_chunk() calls chunk_bytes.decode("utf-8") directly, which crashes when a multi-byte UTF-8 character (e.g. Chinese) is split across HTTP chunk boundaries:
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode bytes in position 65527-65528: unexpected end of data

The repo

vllm.title: [Quantization] Consolidate experts_int8 with fp8 online quantization
vllm.author: Josephasafg
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Following up on this #38032 - This PR Consolidates experts_int8 with fp8's online quantization infrastructure (QeRL). Extracts shared online MoE quantization logic into a common base class and refactors fp8's MoE kernel infrastructure into a reusable mixin.

vllm.author: BadrBasowid
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Adds unit tests for VllmFusionPatternMatcherPass covering:

uuid is stable across instances with the same pattern types and differs for different types.
matched_count and match_table reflect the correct number of matched patterns.

vllm.title: Bugfix: Parakeet: `.conv.pointwise/depthwise_conv1/2.bias weigths` can exist even if `convolution_bias=False`
vllm.author: netanel-haber
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
In transformers v5 (not v4), convolution_bias=False is propagated from parakeet config.
If False, torch.conv1d will skip registering the param, thus it will be missing  in the module's named params.
If you happen to also have the bias tensors in the weights, it will cause a mismatch between the weig

vllm.title: [MLA] Optimize mla indexer prepare uniform decode for MTP > 1
vllm.author: TheEpicDolphin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
While profiling DeepSeek-V3.2 + NVFP4 with MTP > 1 speculative decoding, I noticed that _prepare_decode_tensors in vllm/v1/attention/backends/mla/indexer.py was adding per-step overhead from pytorch ops like repeat_interleave. This function is called once per decode step to build MLA attenti

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Apply ruff formatter to pass pre-commit
vllm.author: Alnusjaponica
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix the pre-commit CI pipeline on the main branch.
The pre-commit CI has been failing since commit 29057d3, which is currently blocking new PRs from being auto-merged. This PR resolves those issues.

Verify that the pre-commit CI passes on this PR.

vllm.title: [UX] Defer some imports on CLI paths to save ~2s
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose


vllm/entrypoints/cli/__init__.py no longer eagerly imports every benchmark subcommand shim.


vllm/benchmarks/sweep/plot.py and plot_pareto.py defer their plotting imports until generating the figure.


vllm/env_override.py drops the explicit torch._inductor.config.compile_threads = 1 line

vllm.title: [Doc] Add Gemma 4 to supported models list
vllm.author: z1ying
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Gemma4ForCausalLM and Gemma4ForConditionalGeneration are already implemented and registered in vLLM (gemma4.py, gemma4_mm.py), but were missing from the supported_models.md documentation table. This PR adds the two missing entries.
Changes
Documentation only — docs/models/supported_models.md
